title: Prediction: Archer Aviation Stock Could Double as FAA Certification Advances
text: 24/7 Wall St. projects Archer Aviation (ACHR) could appreciate significantly as the eVTOL pioneer advances toward FAA certification. The publication issued a BUY rating with $12.01 price target, suggesting 83.67% upside from the $6.54 current price. Archer reported Q1 2026 results showing net loss of $217.70 million on minimal $1.60 million revenue, R&D spending of $171.70 million for FAA certification advancement, cash position of $951.10 million with approximately $1.80 billion total liquidity, and quarterly cash burn of approximately $189 million. The bull case centers on several developments including record FAA Certification Progress advancing to Phase 4, Named Official Air Taxi Provider for LA28 Olympic Games, three eIPP applications spanning eight states, and strategic partnerships with NVIDIA, Palantir, and Starlink. CEO Adam Goldstein stated the company expects to begin initial operations of air taxis in US cities later in 2026. Despite upside potential, analysts flag material risks including finite cash runway given current burn rate, Phase 4 compliance testing with no guaranteed timeline, defense contract awards remaining anticipated rather than confirmed, and share dilution accelerating significantly year-over-year. The analyst recommends buying with moderate confidence contingent on execution with Phase 4 milestones materializing and commercial operations launching in 2026 as guided.
